Video.summary: When you imagine getting an inheritance, will your financial problems be solved or can the money be more trouble than it’s worth?
Video.title: Fighting Over the Will

Video.summary: Imagine hearing voices that don't exist, thinking neighbors were controlling your behavior with magnetic waves, or that people on television were sending you secret messages. What if you constantly thought someone was following you, trying to poison you, out to get you? For those who suffer from schizophrenia, delusions and paranoia are part of daily life. Dr. Phil sheds light on a disease that is both complex and frightening.
Video.title: Schizophrenia

Video.summary: In the last 10 years, the number of women arrested for driving drunk has risen by 30 percent, and police report that more and more young children are sitting in the back seat when these moms are pulled over.
Video.title: Drunk Driving Moms

Video.summary: In part two, the estranged husband of missing mom, Julie Ann Gonzalez, takes a polygraph test.
Video.title: Runaway Moms: The Polygraph

Video.summary: Pedophiles once lurked in parks and around schoolyards, and then they prowled the playground of the World Wide Web. Now these predators have a more sinister method at their fingertips sending explicit text messages directly to your child's cell phone. Dr. Phil examines the latest technological threats to your kids and shows you how to protect them.
Video.title: Pursuing the Predator

Video.summary: Bridget says she’s not sure what to believe about the relationship between her 38-year-old brother-in-law, Kevin, and 16-year-old daughter, Grace.Dr. Phil says Bridget is a parent in denial and in need of a wake-up call. He sits down one-on-one with Kevin. How does Kevin explain his actions?
Video.title: Uncle Kevin Responds (Part 2)

Video.summary: A woman says her ex-husband enables their daughter's addiction.
Video.title: My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child

Image.alt: My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child
My Ex Is Slowly Killing Our Child

Video.summary: This is one powerful episode you can’t miss! Join us as Dr. Phil gives out-of-control mothers the tools they need to curb their offensive behavior. Learn how one single mother’s yelling and offensive language are driving away her teenage kids. Plus, one woman gave up a career to be a stay-at-home mom. Now regret and anxiety have taken over to create a potentially dangerous situation for her children. Whether dealing with teenagers or toddlers, every parent knows that unrelated stress can lead to unwanted outbursts. Learn through these courageous moms how you can be a better parent.
Video.title: Can't Stand Being a Mom

Video.summary: In this powerful program, Dr. Phil exposes the frightening yet underreported problem of mothers on the verge on committing child abuse. We meet ordinary women who live in fear that they’re going to hurt their children in a fit of uncontrollable rage. Chrystel says she feels rage and snaps and then it gets to the point where there's no return. Jessica says her fits of rage become so intense, she's scared that she will hurt her son. Answering their desperate pleas for help, Dr. Phil explains how to identify cues leading up to this precarious state, and tells moms what to do when they start to lose control.
Video.title: Moms on the Verge Part 1

Video.summary: In this powerful program, Dr. Phil once again exposes the frightening yet underreported problem of mothers on the verge of committing child abuse. We meet ordinary women who live with the fear that they’re going to hurt their children in a fit of uncontrollable rage. Joanna says her rage is scaring her son. Michelle’s rage has driven her to slap her child and hit her with a wooden spoon. Lisa admits to pulling her daughter’s hair when she gets angry. Answering their desperate pleas for help, Dr. Phil explains how to identify cues leading up to this precarious state, and tells moms what to do when they start to lose control.
Video.title: Moms on the Verge Part 2

Video.summary: These moms are sick and tired of living a lie. One mom fears her secret addiction to pain killers will tear her family apart. She asks Dr. Phil to help her get off the drugs before she loses everything, including her unborn child! Also, a grandmother who steals everything from steaks to ceiling fans. She says she knows it’s wrong and wants to stop, but is she really just afraid of getting caught? Plus, a mother confesses to her daughter a secret she has been carrying for 29 years.
Video.title: Mom's Secret Life

Video.summary: Being a single mother is hard enough, but imagine juggling soccer practice, PTA meetings and homework ... all while trying to cover up a secret life. Susan is a single mom with two kids who works as a prostitute to pay the bills. She says she's never had a real job and is addicted to the money she makes as an escort. Can Susan ditch her dangerous job and turn her life around? Then, Stephanie is a single mother of four and has been hiding a secret for 12 years. She's addicted to painkillers and takes up to 60 times the recommended dosage just to make it through the day. Fearing for their lives and those of their children, the women turn to Dr. Phil.
Video.title: Secret Lives of Single Moms

Video.summary: Their job is to protect you. They won't let you walk down the street alone. They call your house five times a day, screen your calls, monitor your every move. No, it's not the government or the F.B.I., it's mothers! Is there such a thing as a mom having too much love for her child? Dr. Phil puts Mom to the test!
Video.title: Overprotective Moms

Video.summary: A stay-at-home mother says she can't control her rage, and she takes it out on her kids. Dr. Phil intervenes for the sake of her five precious children.
Video.title: Another Angry Mom

Video.summary: Some moms don't want to grow up, and others don't want to grow old. Dr. Phil talks to women walking on the wild side and tries to rein them back in!
Video.title: Moms Gone Wild

Video.summary: After a Tennessee mother caused an international uproar when she sent her 7-year-old adopted son on a one-way flight back to Russia, the subject of international adoptions is making front-page headlines. Dr. Phil speaks with moms who say they relate to the Tennessee woman, and unless you walk a mile in their shoes, you have no way of understanding. Jane says she had no idea her 2-year-old daughter, Mikhaela, was profoundly deaf and autistic when she adopted her from the Ukraine eight years ago. Jane says the 10-year-old has multiple behavioral disorders and kicks, bites, punches and tries to hurt herself daily, and is getting too strong for her to handle. And, Kristi says all the love in the world couldn’t have helped the baby girl she adopted from China. Kristi says she wasn’t even close to being qualified to parent her now 14-year-old daughter, Tierney, who lives in a behavioral foster home in order to keep Kristi’s other children safe. If you’re thinking of adopting internationally, or if you’re an adoptive parent in crisis, don’t miss today’s show. Learn how to prepare for a cross-cultural adoption, how to get through the adoption process the correct way and what the experts have to say.
Video.title: Adoption: Return to Sender?

Video.summary: Jessica, a mom of five, sent Dr. Phil a home video so disturbing it brought his wife, Robin, and many audience members to tears. Jessica admits that she’s angry all the time and says she struggles to parent her 7-year-old adopted son, Kristoff. She says she screams at the boy, puts hot sauce in his mouth and places him in a cold shower when he acts up. Attorney and child advocate Areva Martin weighs in. Then, several irate women in the audience give Jessica a piece of their mind.
Video.title: Mommy Confessions

Video.summary: In September 2013, Kelli Stapleton and her 14-year-old daughter, Issy -- who suffers from severe autism -- were found in the family van, suffering from carbon monoxide poisoning. Could Kelli have done the unthinkable? In a Dr. Phil exclusive, the jailed Michigan mom opens up about what happened.
Video.title: Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)

Image.alt: Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)
Mom Tried To Kill Autistic Child (Pt. 1)

Video.summary: Dr. Phil continues his exclusive jailhouse interview with Kelli Stapleton, a mother accused of trying to kill herself and her severely autistic 14-year-old daughter, Issy. Were there any warning signs that she was reaching her breaking point? And, find out how you can help Issy secure a healthy and productive future.
Video.title: Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)

Image.alt: Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)
Driven To Try And Kill Her Child? (Pt. 2)

Video.summary: Courtney claims that after her parents’ divorce six years ago, her mother, Michelle, became a “party girl” who basically traded in her children for a life of parties, drinking and men. Courtney says her mother’s dangerous behavior has already resulted in 2 DUIs and a serious car accident that nearly killed her. Courtney says her greatest fear is that “the next time I see my mom will be at her funeral.” Michelle claims that after her divorce, Courtney and her two other children went on with their lives without her, and says that’s why she turned to friends, men and drinking. What does Dr. Phil say Michelle must do to save her life and save her relationship with her children?
Video.title: My Mom Is A Party Girl

Image.alt: My Mom Is A Party Girl
My Mom Is A Party Girl
My Mom Is A Party Girl
My Mom Is A Party Girl
My Mom Is A Party Girl
My Mom Is A Party Girl
My Mom Is A Party Girl
